summary: Diluting a solution keeps the amount of solute constant, so c₁V₁ = c₂V₂. Given any three of the initial and final concentrations and volumes, solve for the fourth.
method: Label the concentrated stock (c₁, V₁) and the diluted solution (c₂, V₂).
Keep concentrations and volumes in consistent units.
Substitute into c₁V₁ = c₂V₂ and solve for the unknown.
